slides.lead: Demonstration deck for this template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to show the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This is where the event title, a one-line subtitle and the meta row at the bottom belong. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Network health, demand data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Bookings, flight sensors and ground systems collect more than anyone can read. Value appears only when it is connected.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: fewer delayed departures after predictive turnaround planning
saved daily on manual schedule reporting
higher seat load factor from smarter pricing
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: what matters is the effect on operations.
Start where you already hold data and feel the pain most.
A small pilot with one clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, plus supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not wait for a delay, it forecasts it.
bullets: Gate timings, weather and crew status from each flight.
What preceded past delays across the network.
Before the knock-on hits later rotations.
Buffers into the slots where they cost the least.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same aircraft, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: We fix delays only once they cascade. Costly and unpredictable.
Buffers set by fixed rules. Better, but often too generous.
Buffers set by real conditions. Lowest total cost.
Critical rotations predictive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a smart airline.
cards: Flight and gate data in real time, close to the source.
Network status and deviations at a glance.
Flags unusual delays before a human spots them.
From raw data straight to a daily ops brief.
Passenger data stays inside your network, under control.
A team that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Improvement slows, so it pays to finish the basics first.
Tools improve fast and reshape established routines.
Automation starts designing routes, not just computing them.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: The airline that wins is not the one with the most data, but the one that turns it into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to show the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Every tool carries the name of the person accountable for it.
You know in advance how success will be recognized.
On safety-critical steps, a person always decides.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A pilot without a metric and an owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build a daily network brief live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title slide. Contact and a call to action.
